message:
fix(frankenterm-core): correct field reference in mux_pool_socket_disappearance assertion message

Adjusts the panic-on-unexpected-Ok message in
crates/frankenterm-core/tests/mux_pool_socket_disappearance.rs to read
the actual `panes.tabs.len()` accessor on the list_panes return type
instead of the non-existent `panes.panes.len()`. The label in the
message text is updated from "pane(s)" to "tab(s)" so the operator-
facing diagnostic matches the underlying field.

This only affects the failure-path message that runs when the test's
core invariant is already violated (list_panes returned Ok after the
socket vanished). The Ok branch was unreachable as written before this
fix because of the missing field; the success path of the test was
unaffected. Two-line change, no logic edits.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
